methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $122M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Halo is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Andrei Iancu that has raised $122M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparable companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including Series A/B/C stage peers such as Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Positron AI ($1.0B), and Substrate ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression to the $122M raised proxy, implying $232M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $1.0B median valuation, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while public semiconductors trade at a 20.6x median price-to-sales across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD, Halo's revenue is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated valuation range of $197M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $616M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Halo's revenue scale and competitive positioning relative to the peer set. Stage-progression suggests a floor near $232M, while peer medians imply substantially higher values. Key unknowns include actual revenue, gross margins, and capital efficiency metrics relative to the named comparables.
